stem: Two identical rectangular prisms each have a height of 60 centimeters (cm). The base of each prism is a square, and the surface area of each prism is K cm². If the prisms are glued together along a square base, the resulting prism has a surface area of (41/21)K cm². What is the side length, in cm, of each square base?
rationale:
Choice C is correct. Each prism has surface area K = 2s² + 4(60)s = 2s² + 240s. When glued along a square base, two faces (each of area s²) are hidden, so the combined surface area is 2(2s² + 240s) − 2s² = 2s² + 480s. Setting 2s² + 480s = (41/21)(2s² + 240s) and solving gives s = 6 cm.

Choices A, B, and D yield incorrect ratios.
